# Runtime Eligibility Evidence

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/runtimeeligibility -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to invariants](../invariants.md)

Executable SSOT: [`runtime-eligibility.riido.json`](runtime-eligibility.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `runtime-eligibility-evidence`.

Proves the C5 pre-submit runtime eligibility gate order and ingress facts from executable scheduling behavior.

## Eligibility Inputs

| Input | Source | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Provider` | TaskRequest.Provider | `task-requirements-provider` |
| `RequiredSurfaces` | TaskRequest.RequiredSurfaces plus metadata fallback | `task-requirements-surfaces` |
| `AllowExperimentalRuntime` | TaskRequest.AllowExperimentalRuntime plus metadata fallback | `task-requirements-experimental` |
| `RuntimeID` | runtime status | `runtime-capability-runtime-id` |
| `CapabilityFingerprint` | C3 capability reconciliation | `runtime-capability-fingerprint` |
| `SlotLimit / SlotsInUse` | runtime status and heartbeat | `runtime-capability-slots` |
| `Available / CompatibilityStatus / RequiresExperimentalOptIn` | C3 capability reconciliation | `runtime-capability-compatibility` |
| `provider-neutral support flags` | C3 capability reconciliation | `runtime-capability-surfaces` |

## Gate Order

| Order | Reason code | Rule | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `PROVIDER_MISMATCH` | task provider and runtime capability provider must match | `gate-provider-mismatch` |
| 2 | `PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE` | unavailable providers are rejected | `gate-unavailable` |
| 3 | `COMPATIBILITY_BLOCKED` | blocked compatibility is rejected | `gate-blocked` |
| 4 | `EXPERIMENTAL_RUNTIME_REQUIRES_OPT_IN` | experimental runtimes require explicit task opt-in | `gate-experimental` |
| 5 | `SLOT_EXHAUSTED` | runtimes with no free slots are rejected | `gate-slot` |
| 6 | `MISSING_REQUIRED_SURFACE` | all required provider-neutral surfaces must be supported | `gate-surface` |

## Ingress Passthroughs

- `supervisor uses C5 gate`: daemon supervisor evaluates taskRequirements through scheduling.EvaluateCapability Evidence: `supervisor-evaluate`.
- `SaaS assignment copies opt-in snapshot`: SaaS assignment AllowExperimentalRuntime is copied into bridge.TaskRequest Evidence: `saas-opt-in-copy`, `saas-opt-in-test`.
- `task DB runtime selection uses scheduling`: local task DB plane selects runtime candidates through scheduling.SelectRuntime Evidence: `taskdb-select-runtime`.

## Forbidden Inference Scans

| Name | Scope | Forbidden tokens | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SaaS opt-in has no env inference | `internal/agentbridge/controlplane/saasplane/task_request_from_assignment.go` | `os.Getenv`, `LookupEnv`, `RIIDO_`, `OPENAI_API_KEY` | SaaS opt-in must come from assignment snapshot, not local daemon environment. |
| SaaS opt-in has no provider/team heuristic | `internal/agentbridge/controlplane/saasplane/task_request_from_assignment.go` | `RuntimeProvider ==`, `TeamID`, `team_id` | SaaS opt-in must not be inferred from provider name or team identity. |

## Assertions

- runtime eligibility must be derived from task requirements and runtime capability snapshot only
- experimental runtime opt-in must be explicit task/assignment data
- surface requirements are evaluated after provider, availability, compatibility, experimental, and slot gates
- reader documentation is generated from this manifest
